slides.title: Writing it down may help your *body heal*.
slides.sub: Putting hard emotions into words can lighten mental load. The effect reaches further than your mood.

slides.title: Name what you feel
slides.body: Expressive writing means honestly describing a difficult experience on paper. The act of *putting it into words* is the whole point.

slides.title: It offloads the load
slides.body: Unspoken stress keeps the mind looping. Writing can *quiet that rumination* and free up attention you did not know was taken.

slides.title: The body responds too
slides.body: In studies, short writing sessions are linked to *measurably faster wound healing* and stronger immune markers.

slides.title: Twenty minutes, four days
slides.body: Write privately about something that weighs on you. A few short sessions *can be enough* to feel the shift.

slides.title: Stress is a health input
slides.body: Emotional load shows up in sleep, recovery and energy. Tending to it *is part of* tending to your body.

slides.title: Watch your habits *add up*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the small things you do for your body stay visible.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Writing about a hard emotion does more than clear your head. It can ripple into how your body recovers. 🌿

Naming the stress quiets the mental loop, and short, private writing sessions are linked to faster healing and stronger immune markers. Save this for a heavy week. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Writing About Your Feelings Helps You Heal
